response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack development, a strong TypeScript/Node.js/React core, and — critically — active AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic frameworks) at their current role, which directly satisfies one of the harder-to-find requirements. Database (MongoDB, MySQL), cloud (AWS CDK), and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) boxes are checked. However, Nest.js — the primary backend framework — is entirely absent from the resume, as are Kotlin, Python, Vitest, and Playwright, all of which are explicitly required. The gap between the candidate's demonstrated stack and the target stack is wide enough to warrant a screening call before committing to a full interview loop.
strengths: Active AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at current employer directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Long polyglot full-stack career with production TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK across multiple companies
Demonstrated testing craft: TDD practitioner, authored a D-language test runner and assertion library, ran public testing workshops
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework in the required stack — is not mentioned anywhere in the resume
Kotlin and Python are listed as required proficiencies but are absent from all work history and skills sections
Vitest and Playwright are not referenced; security and authentication practices are not explicitly described in any role

key_factors.factor: TypeScript / Node.js / React depth with cloud and containerization
